trade.summary: France and Italy are among each other's most important trading partners as EU neighbours and eurozone members. Trade spans machinery, automobiles, agri-food, chemicals, and fashion. Italy is France's third-largest export market. Supply chains are deeply integrated.

diplomatic.summary: France and Italy are founding EU members with deep integration, but political friction has emerged under Italy's Meloni government. Disagreements over migration policy, African engagement, and Libya create friction. The 2021 Quirinal Treaty formalized cooperation. Relations remain fundamentally stable within the EU framework.
recent_incidents: Franco-Italian dispute over migrant boat policies (2022-2024)
rivalry in Africa and Sahel policy

military.summary: Both NATO allies with close defence industrial ties. Joint cooperation in the Eurodrone programme, MGCS tank project involvement, and Mediterranean maritime operations. Italian and French navies cooperate in NATO and EU naval missions. Some competition in arms exports to third markets.

financial.summary: Significant cross-border investment. French companies are among the largest foreign investors in Italy, including in banking, utilities, and retail. Italian companies invest substantially in France. Some friction over French acquisition of Italian firms (Telecom Italia, Mediaset).

energy.summary: Energy cooperation through shared electricity grids and joint participation in EU energy market. France supplies nuclear electricity to Italy. Cooperation on Mediterranean pipeline infrastructure and diversification away from Russian gas post-2022.

scientific.summary: Extensive academic collaboration through EU research frameworks, joint universities, and bilateral agreements. Strong cooperation in physics (CERN), space (ESA), and life sciences. Many researchers move between the two countries.
